user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
not necessary.
spot up/vol up is created by negative speed environment, meaning the gamma convexity to the upside is negative.
so as the spot goes up, MMs gamma exposure decreases, or even goes into negative territory. Less and less price has less and less resistance, or in net short gamma environment, dealers start to hedge into its direction, and it basically leads to the expansion of the intraday + daily returns, making RV to print higher.
(bcs what is volatility?...)

also the positioning is basically a short skew from customer side, so as spot grinds higher (to the right side of the x-axis on a skew chart), the right wing is getting steeper relative to the put wing.
IV up.
dealers are long skew here, so they cover the upside vol by buying more upside vol (short vanna environment) as they become shorter vol as spot moves up.
this is a gamma squeeze basically.
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
(for beginners: skew is the IV across the strikes. x-axis: strikes, y-axis: iv values. 
iv is indirectly the supply/demand on a certain strike contract, bcs iv is the vol risk premium sellers ask. Higher demand = higher IV)
comment.raw_body:
(for beginners: skew is the IV across the strikes. x-axis: strikes, y-axis: iv values. 
iv is indirectly the supply/demand on a certain strike contract, bcs iv is the vol risk premium sellers ask. Higher demand = higher IV)

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
(and vanna is basically the skewness of this seesaw/skew:
long skew = OTM puts up relative to OTM calls = short vanna
short skew = OTM puts down relative to OTM calls = long vanna)
comment.raw_body:
(and vanna is basically the skewness of this seesaw/skew:
long skew = OTM puts up relative to OTM calls = short vanna
short skew = OTM puts down relative to OTM calls = long vanna)
